name: Zerodha Order Service — Kubernetes Deployment
scenario: Zerodha needs to deploy order-service to Kubernetes for market-open traffic handling. Write all required k8s manifests and Spring Boot probe configuration for zero-downtime deployments.

core.description: k8s/deployment.yml: 3 replicas, resources (512Mi/768Mi, 250m/500m), rolling update (maxSurge=1, maxUnavailable=0), readiness+liveness probes.
k8s/service.yml: ClusterIP, port 80 → 8080, selector app: order-service, namespace: trading.
k8s/hpa.yml: CPU target 60%, min 2, max 20 replicas.
application.yml: management.health.probes.enabled=true. Liveness: livenessState only. Readiness: readinessState + db.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): readiness endpoint 2xx, liveness returns UP status, /actuator/prometheus contains jvm_memory. All 3 pass.
README: kubectl commands to deploy, scale, and rollback.

bonus.description: PodDisruptionBudget: minAvailable=2. Document how it prevents downtime during node drain.
PodAntiAffinity: distribute pods across different nodes (preferredDuringScheduling). Explain HA benefit.
NetworkPolicy: order-service can only be called from specific source labels. Block all other ingress.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. management.health.probes.enabled=true: required for /actuator/health/readiness and /liveness (without this, both are the same as /health). Readiness probe initialDelaySeconds should be >= Spring Boot startup time (~25-30s); liveness initialDelaySeconds should be longer (60s) to avoid restarting a healthy but slow-starting app. Never include DB in liveness group — DB failure → liveness failure → restart → DB still down → infinite CrashLoopBackOff. maxUnavailable=0: zero downtime guarantee (k8s never terminates an old pod until a new one is ready). Resources: requests = scheduling guarantee; limits = hard ceiling. 250m CPU = 0.25 cores.
